summary: End-to-end testing of AI Lyricist MVP. Backend: all 19 pytest cases passed (19/19) covering root, providers, styles CRUD (incl. preset protection), songs upload + librosa analysis + CRUD + audio streaming + soft delete, drafts versioning/approve/delete, lyrics generate/complete/polish (anthropic cloud), providers probe, 502 on bogus ollama. Frontend: Studio upload→analysis→generate→save/approve flow verified; Songbook list + search + BPM filter verified; SongDetail shows version history with v1 and v2 FINAL markers; Styles page list/create verified; Providers/Settings page shows FAILED status for unreachable ollama/lmstudio endpoints.

issues: BPM shown as '—' for a 2-second synthetic clip; acceptable since librosa cannot reliably detect BPM on <~4s clips. Consider showing 'n/a (clip too short)' hint.

critical_code_review_comments: StylesPage delete uses window.confirm — blocks Playwright auto-dismiss by default and is inconsistent with the rest of the app using shadcn AlertDialog. Recommend replacing with AlertDialog for consistency and testability.
Studio toasts fire from background tasks after navigation (noticed 'Instrumental analyzed' appearing on new pages). Minor UX nit; not a bug.
server.py startup uses deprecated @app.on_event — migrate to FastAPI lifespan context manager before future FastAPI upgrades.
server.py upload_song: librosa first-call ~20-40s is blocking; consider offloading to BackgroundTasks or thread executor to avoid holding an async worker.
Consider rate-limiting /api/lyrics/* and enforcing max must_have_words / structure length to guard against prompt abuse.
server.py lines grow — currently ~615 lines; nearing the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting routers by resource (songs / drafts / styles / lyrics).
test_credentials: No auth required (single-user demo mode). EMERGENT_LLM_KEY in backend/.env.
seed_data_creation: Created /tmp/test.wav (2-second 22.05kHz synthetic WAV using numpy+soundfile) for upload tests. Preset styles seeded automatically on backend startup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (pytest, 19 cases). Run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v. The test creates a small WAV at /tmp/test.wav via the uploaded_song fixture — if missing, regenerate with: python3 -c 'import numpy as np, soundfile as sf; sr=22050; t=np.linspace(0,2,2*sr,endpoint=False); y=0.2*np.sin(2*np.pi*440*t); [y.__setitem__(slice(int(b*sr), int(b*sr)+1000), y[int(b*sr):int(b*sr)+1000]+0.5*np.hanning(1000)) for b in np.arange(0,2,0.5)]; sf.write("/tmp/test.wav",y,sr)'. Cloud LLM calls take ~10-20s so keep timeouts >=120s. Frontend style delete uses window.confirm — Playwright must page.on('dialog', lambda d: d.accept()) to actually trigger DELETE. AppHeader nav testids: nav-studio, nav-songbook, nav-styles, nav-providers (which routes to /settings).
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Note: during Playwright testing the style-delete button did not reduce row count because StylesPage.jsx uses native window.confirm() which Playwright dismisses by default. Backend DELETE /api/styles/{id} is verified working by pytest; UI delete itself works manually. Recommend migrating to shadcn AlertDialog for consistency and deterministic testability.
